@Adam - have you tried to encode JSON file? Maybe it will be easier to follow such path

add below
e.g. kubectl edit secret aaf-cert-service-secret

and remove comment

add below
e.g. kubectl exec -it (name under which CertService starts when is installed via OOM) bash

Add following sentence as first and the only one in Overview:
In Frankfurt release AAF was enhanced by Certificate Management Protocol ver. 2 (CMPv2) support. Such support is handled by new AAF's microservice called CertService. CertService provides certificates signed by external CMPv2 server - further on such certificates are called operators certificates. Operators certificates are meant to secure external ONAP traffic - traffic between network functions (xNFs) and ONAP.
Move four paragraphs (From the micro-service.... to To proof) to Architecture chapter (above Interaction between components).
Move this paragraph to Context View above picture
Before this sentence add subchapter called Supported Funtionality

All Certification Service -> CertService
All Certification Service Client -> CertService client

Parameters to generate Certificate Signing Request (CSR):
local path to keystore in JKS format
local path to truststore in JKS format
Link doesn't point to logs from client side. Please fix this

following? Really - I thought that this is an example and every component has to provide its own configuration for CSR - please include such information
deployment
We can use env variable for password, but not for paths, because they are not stored in ENV variables in docker-compose deployment. Should I use env for password?

It's possible to store configuration in file (e.g. sample-config.json) and encode it with the command:
echo sample-config.json | base64

But it will require additional step (creating a file).
However it be easier to modify configuration later on.

Do you want me to change it to the described approach?

I had a problem with that, because global values can be overwritten in multiple places (parent charts, override files or even during deployment). I'd leave it as it is, global prefix indicates that it's global value, so if we assume that user knows kubernetes, he knows where he should change it.

I'd change section description to "Parameters to establish secure communication to CertService"
and leave ENVs as it is, because they are self explanatory in that scope.
What do you think?
Yes, use as many ENVs as possible. Especially that e.g. passwords can be different if operator provides own secret.
Not acceptable. This is not K8s, but helm so we must be precise in which file it should be checked.

Yes Helm, my bad. However to be honest I don't know which file should be checked, because this can be even override in multiple places as I mentioned. Should I point to 
kubernetes/onap/values.yaml? However changing it there does not guarantee that will be the value used..
